unassociated.description: Devices sending probe requests without being connected to any AP

whitelist.title: Whitelist Management
whitelist.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete?

settings.graph_stepped_help: Stepped graphs have a blockier representation of the same data. By default, graphs are displayed in linear mode, which typically shows a more sloped representation of changes.
settings.login_p1: Kismet requires a username and password for functionality which changes the server, such as adding interfaces or changing configuration, or accessing some types of data.
settings.login_p2: The Kismet password is stored in ~/.kismet/kismet_httpd.conf in the home directory of the user running Kismet. You will need this password to configure data sources, download pcap and other logs, or change server-side settings.
settings.login_p3: If you are a guest on this server you may continue without entering an admin password, but you will not be able to perform some actions or view some data.
settings.login_path: This server is running as {{user}}, so the password can be found in ~{{user}}/.kismet/kismet_httpd.conf
settings.api_token_disabled: Viewing auth tokens is disabled in the Kismet configuration.
settings.api_role_unknown: Unknown role {{role}}; this could be assigned as a custom role for a Kismet plugin.
settings.api_role_admin: The admin role is assigned to the primary web interface, external API plugins which automatically request API access, and other privileged instances. The admin role has access to all endpoints.
settings.api_role_readonly: The readonly role has access to any endpoint which does not modify data. It cannot issue commands to the Kismet server, configure sources, or alter devices. The readonly role is well suited for external data gathering from a Kismet server.
settings.api_role_datasource: The datasource role allows remote capture over websockets. This role only has access to the remote capture datasource endpoint.
settings.api_role_scanreport: The scanreport role allows device scan reports. This role only has access to the scan report endpoint.
settings.api_role_adsb: The ADSB role allows access to the combined and device-specific ADSB feeds.
settings.api_roles_explain_body:
Kismet uses a basic role system to restrict access to API endpoints. The default roles are:

"admin" which has access to all API endpoints.

"readonly" which only has access to endpoints which do not alter devices or change the configuration of the server

"datasource" which is used for websockets based remote capture and may not access any other endpoints

"scanreport" which is used for reporting scanning-mode devices

"ADSB" which is used for sharing ADSB feeds

Plugins or other code may define other roles.
settings.radiation_caution: CAUTION: Radiation exposure counts and dosages are dependent on the radiation detector used. Most radiation detectors have maximum speeds at which detection can reliably occur. Each radiation detector must be properly calibrated and properly oriented for detection.
settings.radiation_safe: BE SAFE. NEVER trust the Kismet display of radiation data for personal safety!
settings.radiation_show_cps: Show counts per second in toolbar
settings.radiation_show_usv: Show uSv per second in toolbar
settings.unsaved_prompt: Would you like to save the changes?
settings.whitelist_column_desc: Whether the device MAC is on the whitelist (icon)

common.select_rows_first: Select one or more rows from the list first.
